desc:
Certain portions of the ground are solid sandstone, whereas other parts are
composed of loose dirt topped by yellow grass blades, struggling to survive.
Northwest, in the distance, a huge cliff can be seen. Stretching out towards the
east a proud forest of trees grows blanketed by a misty air.
name: Mountains

desc:
In the distant west, a dark cliff grows ominously, but here, only a few pebbles
dot the moist ground. For the most part, the ground harbours a few green grasses
and a modest bit of moss. Yellow stones look as if they had been tossed about
the area randomly. To the north, there rises a wall of trees not higher than
three men, forming a dark canopy of grey and green.
name: Forest Border

desc:
The ground here slopes down towards the northeast. The rich earth that covers
the ground has been swept away, indicating that perhaps this area was once a
riverbed in ages past. The tree roots crawl like fingers into the abandoned
riverbed and then burrow into the dry earth. From the north and east the sound
of rushing water can faintly be heard.
name: Forest along the Entwash Bend

desc:
A patch of rowans has started to grow here upon the hill. Through the trees and
ashen mist one can make out the branches that spring out of the low lying trunks
of these proud rowans. Most miraculous of all however, is how these slender
branches weave up into the canopy, delicate yet strong. Covering the ground
between the gnarled roots grows a variety of moss and mushrooms, taking
advantage of the uncomfortable humidity.
name: Rowan Grove

desc:
A creeping mist flows over the roots that cover the ground. It swirls about the
trees and stifles everything in the moist air. From out of the ground rise giant
boles with trunks that stretch up until their branches become entangled and form
parts of a canopy that chokes out the light from above. Moss and ferns grow
along the ground, speckled with dew.
name: Misty Forest

desc:
The ground drops significantly to the south. Giant trees with interlocked roots
grow at an angle to the ground with their majestic trunks reaching towards the
sky. Small plants litter the ground, trying to prevent erosion. Roots line the
slope southwards as well, making the passage up or down a bit easier. The sound
of water can be heard from the northeast.
name: Forest along the Entwash

desc:
Washed rocks and pebbles cover the ground as a small tributary of the Entwash
rushes down the hill into the grey and misty forest beyond. The ground rises to
an open, grass-covered hill to the west. It is covered with rich yellow and
green blades of grass, but there also grow two proud and dark polished pines. It
slopes southward into a significantly less dense forest composed of deciduous
trees.
name: Lofty Hill along the Entwash

desc:
Two great trees stand here, one on each side of the hill, like living
gate-posts. The two proud evergreens are polished dark, and gleam in the light
that makes its way from the sky. From beneath their boughs and roots, the river
Entwash splashes at a slow pace from a hidden location.
name: Dark Pines by the Entwash

desc:
Beyond the dark pines to the east, there lies a wide level space, as though the
floor of a great hall had been cut in the side of the hill. Two walls follow the
level space on either side, growing in height. On each side of the path, there
sits an aisle of trees that grow in size as they progress towards the back of
the hall. Running from the west, a small tributary of the Entwash river gurgles
its way down the slope towards the east.
name: Small River in the Glade

desc:
Trees of various species grow here at medium height and gradually get taller as
they progress westwards towards the rock-face. The forest floor consists of a
multitude of green ferns, grasses, and mosses that patch the brown damp earth.
The slow rustle of water can be heard from the north.
name: Forest along the Precipice

desc:
A rock face that reaches about fifty feet stands here, its sheer face made up of
solid yellow granite. The trees have grown to an enormous height and their
canopy nearly blocks out all light from above. To the east, the forest starts to
shrink in height and a slight rustle of water can be heard towards the large
level area in the north.
name: Grove in the Fangorn Forest

desc:
The end of the path has now stopped at the rock wall that stretches upwards for
fifty feet or more. At the bottom, it has been hollowed back into a shallow bay
with an arched roof of stone. Far above, a spring pours silver drops of water
down upon the face of the rock wall and spills like a fine curtain in front of
the arched bay. The water gathers in a stone basin in the floor and then flows
out beside the open path, down towards the east.
name: Glade Facing the Rock-wall

desc:
A large, hollowed out bay has been dug out of the rock-wall. Its arched roof
makes a perfect turn about the cave for fifteen or more feet into the air. The
inside of the bay is considerably dimmer than the outside, but a silver curtain
of water that rushes down the outside of the bay gives a feeling of warmth to
the room with its cleared basin and large stone structures.
name: Wellinghall

desc:
A sheer rock face now tells the end of the forest that grows within this hewn
hall of granite. Reaching fifty feet into the air it surpassed all the trees of
many forms that grow here within the hall. The damp earth that harbours plants,
mosses, and ferns has been locked within a matrix of roots that then springs
upwards into the tallest of the trees of this forest aisle.
name: Grove in the Fangorn Forest

desc:
The forest here grows well. Its roots stretch across the damp earth that
harbours a myriad of fungi, mosses, and grasses alongside the giant trees that
grow upwards in many slants and forms. As the forest progresses further towards
the east, the sheer rock wall follows on the north, growing in size directly
proportional to the surrounding forest. From the south, a slight whisper of
water can be heard trickling away.
name: Forest along the Precipice

desc:
Giant roots criss-cross along the ground, shattering their way into the earth
and causing the forest floor to be littered with pebbles and debris. The canopy
nearly shuts out all light in the massive tangle of grey branches found above.
Still, the absence of light is joined by the sound of water towards the south,
where the limestone bedrock is exposed to the air. The northern direction seems
to be blocked by many closely growing pine trees.
name: Forest by the Entwash

desc:
A misty grey forest, composed of gnarled trees with an interlocking canopy of
boughs and leaves, grows in near silence. A giant rock structure lies to the
south, gouged with scrapes and nooks. Grey roots stream across the ground and
anchor themselves into the mossy, twig covered forest floor of green and grey
tones. The northern direction is blocked by several ancient trees that seem to
have grown closely together for ages, in a west-east leading row.
name: Dim Forest

desc:
The soil here has been scraped away, exposing rough limestone along the moist,
twig covered ground. Though the limestone is hard, tree roots easily pierce
their way through the stone rather than grow around. Through time the limestone
has become more solid woven between age-old roots. The sound of water can be
heard trickling away faintly.
name: Forest along the Entwash

desc:
Broken bits of limestone cover the ground as roots dig their way into the earth
along the forest floor. The roots grind through all sorts of forest debris,
littering the ground and heading in a vaguely southward direction. However,
without apparent reason, everything seems to drift to this direction. The sound
of water can barely be heard from the south, perhaps giving some reason to the
southerly descent.
name: Forest by the Entwash

desc:
Perception is entirely limited to three yards in any direction. Moist mossy and
brown boles grow from the ground, stretching upward with immense power. A giant
root slowly rises in a large arch before grinding into the limestone bedrock.
Within the arch grows rows upon rows of orange mushrooms among a variety of
moist mosses and green ferns.
name: Forest next to the Entwash

desc:
Sun-tanned stones of a light brown shade clutter the ground, locked in piles.
Around them, the moist rich soil harbours moss and green grasses. The ridge
rises toward the snow-capped mountains, turning slightly with the land contours.
To the east the ground slowly seems to drop, and a forest is seen shrouded in a
grey mist.
name: Rising Ridge

desc:
A towering forest lies to the east with its branches reaching an enormous
height. Grey bark and gnarled roots make up most of what can be seen in that
direction. Far above, the branches consist of an entangled mess of differently
nuanced leaves. A brown ridge with some spots of green foliage grows toward the
white mountains to the east.
name: Forest Boundary

desc:
A giant cliff of rock towers in the east, with boulders and giant slabs lying
around at different angles at its base. To the south lies a cliff of black rock
with a slab propped up against it. Grasses grow interweaving between the rubble
that litters the ground. The ridge seems to rise yet more to the west and
onwards to the north.
name: Rising Ridge

desc:
The ridge slows its ascent and becomes nearly straight. Yellow and brown rocks
make up clumped formations around this area. As the ridge trails onward, the
rocks become larger and jut out of the ground to form an unclimbable maze.
Boulders cover the ground, blocking passage to the south and west. To the north
and east there is a rising ridge.
name: Boulder Ridge

desc:
Jagged rocks cover the dirt floor. The ceiling is composed of the same grey rock
that makes up the giant slab for the entrance. The roof rises in an uneven arch
until reaching the back wall of the cave. The slow drip of water can be heard
from somewhere, carried along by a cold draft.
name: Small Cave

desc:
Flowing from the west, the Entwash swiftly glides over rocks and pebbles.
Rippling and ever in motion, it flows on towards the east into the mist. Above,
a grey canopy covers the sky as branches struggle to gain access to any, even
the most scant, rays of light. Rising sharply, the river bank of cracked
limestone grows a few feet into the air, where tree roots dig their way down
into the river.
name: River Entwash

desc:
Cold water rushes down from the west to the east. Farther along the river, the
water quietly trickles around rocks making many perfect ripples across its
surface. At the top of the limestone bank lays a wall of brown and grey tree
boles. They reach upward, each tree covered in moss forming a thick canopy of
boughs and leaves that shuts out any light from above.
name: River Entwash

desc:
Crystal clear water gurgles smoothly down the streambed, gliding in from the
west and flowing south. It moves gracefully over the stones until it reaches one
of the many rocks that it cannot completely rise over, and thus splashes into
the air. Grey limestone, worn and old, makes up the bank, upon which a snarl of
roots hints at the immense and thick forest that towers above.
name: Bend in the Entwash

desc:
The water flows from the north and then on towards the east. It glides over
river-worn pebbles and scrapes against the limestone that makes up the looming
bank of the river. Trees, that grow into the sky, smother nearly all light from
above with their branches forming an intricate web of leaves and twigs. From
their boles reach roots of enormous size that grow down into the river.
name: Bend in the Entwash

desc:
Despite the utter darkness of this part of the forest, the undergrowth is quite
developed, although the leaves appear to be in shades of grey and black, rather
than the usual greens. This peculiar vegetation even seems to thrive on
darkness, as the rare places where a bit of sunlight breaks through at times,
are strangely deserted. In every direction, large dark trunks, almost regularly
spaced out, stretch out in the far distance, like the bars of a colossal
portcullis. But the real hindrance comes from all the sharp-leaved black bushes
overgrowing in the undergrowth.
name: Utterly Dark Forest

desc:
The floor, covered by ice which was left when the subterranean river vanished,
is very slick, making it difficult to move without sliding towards the east,
which is the direction of the natural slant of this tunnel. The air here is
terribly cold, in spite of a less chilling breeze coming from a conduit above.
The husk of a worm-like creature is visible here.
name: Rounded Tunnel

desc:
The smooth tunnel makes a bend here, where the river followed a vein of softer
rocks towards the south. The floor of the tunnel is made slippery by a thin
pellicle of ice. A chilling murmur comes out of a large opening in the north
wall along with an ice-cold draught. Dry husks of strange worm-like creatures
litter the floor.
name: Opening in Rounded Tunnel

desc:
In this section of the tunnel, a slow mass of stinking earth flows westwards as
it follows the slope of an old river, almost blocking the passage in that
direction. The dirty earth, along with a foul dark liquid, oozes from a black
hole in the ceiling high above filling the air with its polluted stench of urea.
name: Circular Tunnel

desc:
This rounded tunnel, its walls polished by the water which flowed through it
centuries ago, follows a twisted path through the mountain. Now the stream is
gone, probably frozen by the incredible cold which inhabits these chambers.
name: Circular Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel slants downwards to the west where its polished walls still bear the
marks of the river which created them years ago. The water, hurling down from an
impressive vertical shaft just above your head, created the unclimbable,
silky-smooth walls. North of you, a recent seism has caused a vertical crack to
open in the rock. Dangling above the crack you can see the remnants of a small
spider-web.
name: Small Circular Tunnel

desc:
Sliding and ramping, crawling and climbing, you can slowly make your way through
the twisted crack, scratching your members at the etched edges. The stones that
you touch are warmer than one might expect in these surroundings and are covered
by a thin pellicle of gluey salivation. Above you, the fissure is closed by a
enormous web, in which the dry remains and bones of humanoids are still fixed.
name: In the Crack

desc:
The crack is some five to ten yards high and forms a ragged set of teeth where
the granite was ripped apart. The hand and foot holds that allow one to drop
down into the crack unscathed are just a little too high to allow to climb out.
name: In the Crack

desc:
The roof of what was once a large cavern lies partially crumbled on the floor.
While the exact events that made the roof crumble are unknown, the condition of
the walls indicates that the forces involved must have been tremendous and
spontaneous. Broken boulders are piled upon the ground, forcing any traveller
through this area to climb again and again while picking the way through the
desolation.
name: A Dislocated Cavern

desc:
This niche in the cavern has been demolished by an underground tremor causing
many rocks and boulders to fall on the floor. The collapse of the ceiling has
obstructed the path to the west, as several huge boulders have piled against a
natural granite pillar. The smell coming out of the furthest corner indicates
that death is never far.
name: A Small Dislocated Cave

desc:
AAA AAA AAA AAA AAA AAA AAA AAA AAA AH! CROUNK! BONG! SPLASH! You would have
broken every bone in your body if the pit had been empty, but instead you
drowned in the twenty feet of urine which covers the bottom of the pit. Some
people have had more pleasant deaths.
name: A Dark Bottomless Pit

desc:
You find yourself in a flat segment of a circular tunnel, smooth and rounded as
if the tunnel had been artificially built. However, common sense would indicate
that this tunnel was formed by an underground river. The yellow- brownish walls
have been moulded into the basalt rocks and polished by age. Small spider-webs
can be seen hanging in the corners, a clear indication that in spite of the
coldness and deepness of these tunnels, there is enough food to sustain some
forms of life. The floor is littered by husks of strange worm-like creatures.
name: Rounded Tunnel

desc:
This place is an ancient confluence of two underground rivers, one coming from
the north and another from the east. These rivers once joined here forming a
torrent that hurled down into the deep pit to the south. While the flowing water
vanished long ago, a rivulet of dark liquid mixed with soiled dirt still flows
slowly in its place, filling the tunnel with a terrible stench.
name: Rounded Tunnel

desc:
Deciduous trees rise up into the sky from the moist, sandstone covered ground.
Their roots creep around the solid boulders and dig into the soil that harbours
yellow and green grasses. The view north and west is dominated by solid walls of
nearly vertical dark stone. Towards the south, the trees disperse, and the
ground starts to rise.
name: Forest Edge

desc:
Medium sized trees rise from the ground, barely reaching above the height of
three men. The trees are not nearly as dense as others towards the north or
east, but the foliage grows significantly better due to the amount of light
available. The ground is covered with hard yellow sandstone, limiting growth of
the trees and allowing an abundance of foliage that sits in the comfort of the
shade.
name: Forest by the Entwash

desc:
The trees make a giant transition here. To the west they begin to disperse, yet
here they grow with more vigour. Giant roots strangle the earth with their grip,
pulverizing the limestone that shows up in certain locations. Small types of
flora grow on the forest ground in many shades of brilliant purples, giving some
colour to the blandness of the grey trees and misty air.
name: Forest by the Entwash
